title: Care Readiness Assessment
summary: 8 minutes · Results are informational, not a diagnosis

questions.question: How often does your parent forget to take medication or misses doses?
options: I'm not sure — no one's tracking it

questions.question: How is your parent managing meals and groceries?
Has your parent had any falls or near-falls recently?
How would you describe your parent's home environment?
How connected does your parent seem day to day?
options: Rarely leaves the house or has visitors

results.title: Your parent seems to be managing well
results.body: Based on your answers, day-to-day things are largely under control right now. This is a good time to put a few light safeguards in place before anything feels urgent.

results.title: You're noticing the early signs
results.body: Based on your answers, your parent seems mostly independent, with a few things worth keeping an eye on. This is a great time to start a light plan, before anything feels urgent.

results.title: A few things are worth addressing soon
results.body: Based on your answers, there are several areas — safety, medication, or daily support — that would benefit from more active attention in the near term.

nextSteps: Read "Signs a Parent May Need Help" to understand what to watch for
Start a simple family caregiving plan while things are calm
Check back in with this assessment in a few months
disclaimer: This assessment is informational only and isn't a medical diagnosis. If you have urgent concerns, contact your parent's doctor.
